notable_pick_trades: Traded Rashid Shaheed to SEA during 2025 season for 4th and 5th round picks (132, 172). Sent 2026 4th to DEN for WR Devaughn Vele in August 2025. Received compensatory 4th (136).
roster_context: The Saints finished 6-11 in Kellen Moore's first year as HC but found their franchise QB in second-round pick Tyler Shough, who led the team to a 5-3 record in his 8 starts. The 2026 offseason is about building around Shough — the OL tackles are set with back-to-back first-rounders Kelvin Banks Jr. and Taliese Fuaga, and the team made splash FA signings (Etienne, Edwards, Elliss, Fant). But the defense lost three major pieces: Demario Davis (LB to Jets), Alontae Taylor (CB to Titans), and Cameron Jordan (EDGE, still unsigned UFA). WR remains thin behind Chris Olave. Cap situation is still tight but improving.

needs.explanation: After trading Rashid Shaheed to Seattle mid-season in 2025, the Saints have no proven WR2 behind Chris Olave. Devaughn Vele projects as the WR2 but is a former 7th-round pick who was underutilized for much of 2025. Ja'Lynn Polk was acquired from New England but was on IR the entire 2025 season. Trey Palmer, Ronnie Bell, and others are fringe roster players. Free agency was intentionally left untouched at WR because the draft class is elite. Tyler Shough needs weapons to take the next step.
needs.depth: Ja'Lynn Polk (returning from IR), Trey Palmer, Ronnie Bell (RFA), Mason Tipton (RFA) — very thin after Olave
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1 (pick 8) — Carnell Tate or Makai Lemon are consensus mocks

needs.explanation: Cameron Jordan (36, 10.5 sacks in 2025) hit free agency for the first time in 15 years and remains unsigned. Even if Jordan returns on a cheap deal, the Saints need a long-term pass rush partner for Chase Young. Carl Granderson had a down 2025 season. The team has not signed an edge rusher in free agency, leaving a clear hole for the draft or second-wave FA.
needs.depth: Fadil Diggs (2025 7th-round pick), Cameron Jordan (UFA, future uncertain)
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-2 — Rueben Bain Jr. or Gabe Jacas are commonly mocked

needs.explanation: Alontae Taylor, who played the critical 'star' position in Brandon Staley's defense (the same role Derwin James and Jalen Ramsey filled for Staley), signed a 3-year, $60M deal with the Titans. This is a massive loss. Kool-Aid McKinstry showed improvement in his second year (NFC Defensive Player of the Week in Week 5) and is the CB1, while 2025 4th-rounder Quincy Riley projects as CB2. But the slot/star role is vacant and needs addressing.
needs.depth: Isaac Yiadom, Isaiah Stalbird (versatile) — star/nickel role vacant after Taylor departure
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-3 — Caleb Downs (S/star hybrid) has been linked at pick 8

needs.explanation: Justin Reid is under contract on a 3-year deal and is the starter, but he had an inconsistent 2025 season and will be 29. Tyrann Mathieu retired before the 2025 season, and 2025 3rd-round pick Jonas Sanker stepped into the starting role as a rookie. Sanker showed promise but is still developing. The secondary needs another playmaker, especially with Taylor's departure — a versatile safety/slot hybrid like Caleb Downs could address both the star and safety needs.
needs.depth: Limited beyond starters; secondary depth is a concern

needs.explanation: Demario Davis, a franchise cornerstone and team captain for years, signed with the Jets in free agency. The Saints replaced him with Kaden Elliss (3-year, $33M), a former homegrown product returning from Atlanta. Pete Werner and 2025 4th-rounder Danny Stutsman are also in the mix. Elliss is a solid starter with 100+ tackle seasons, but the room lost significant leadership and the depth behind the top three is thin.

Bryan Bresee, a former first-round pick, has struggled mightily in the NFL (37.3 PFF grade in 2024). Vernon Broughton was drafted in the 3rd round in 2025 but missed most of the season with injury. John Ridgeway was re-signed as a depth run stuffer. Nathan Shepherd and Davon Godchaux are replacement-level players who are possible cut candidates. The interior line needs an infusion of talent.
needs.depth: Vernon Broughton (returning from injury), Davon Godchaux, Nathan Shepherd — Bresee has underperformed

needs.explanation: The David Edwards signing (4-year, $61M) was the offseason's top guard acquisition and fills the massive LG hole. Erik McCoy returns as starter at center but has missed 20 of his last 34 games due to injuries. Luke Fortner, the backup C who started 10 games in 2025, signed with the Panthers. Center depth behind the injury-prone McCoy is now a concern. Cesar Ruiz at RG is adequate but could be upgraded.
needs.current_starter: David Edwards (LG), Erik McCoy (C), Cesar Ruiz (RG)
needs.depth: Dillon Radunz (re-signed as swing OL), Asim Richards, Torricelli Simpkins III — backup center is a specific gap after Fortner departure

free_agency_impact: Signed RB Travis Etienne (4yr/$52M from JAX) — RB no longer a top draft need, reduces urgency for Jeremiyah Love at 8
Signed G David Edwards (4yr/$61M from BUF) — fills critical LG hole, IOL drops to depth-only need
Signed LB Kaden Elliss (3yr/$33M from ATL) — partially replaces Demario Davis at LB, reduces urgency
Signed TE Noah Fant (2yr deal from CIN) — addresses TE need after Foster Moreau departed to HOU and Jack Stoll to CLE
Signed P Ryan Wright (from MIN) — replaces traded Kai Kroeger, P no longer a need
Re-signed DT John Ridgeway (2yr/$6.2M) — adds run-stuffing depth on DL
Re-signed OL Dillon Radunz — swing OL depth
Lost CB Alontae Taylor (3yr/$60M to TEN) — MAJOR loss, creates CB/star need
Lost LB Demario Davis (to NYJ) — massive leadership void, partially filled by Elliss
Lost C Luke Fortner (1yr/$4.75M to CAR) — backup C depth now a concern given McCoy's injury history
Lost TE Foster Moreau (to HOU) — addressed with Fant signing
Lost TE Jack Stoll (to CLE) — further depleted TE room before Fant signing
Cameron Jordan (EDGE, 36) — UFA, still unsigned, likely leaving or returning on team-friendly deal; Saints absorbed $18.76M dead cap from voided contract
Taysom Hill — future uncertain, contemplating retirement
Tyrann Mathieu retired before 2025 season — already addressed with Jonas Sanker stepping up
scheme_fit_notes: Brandon Staley's defense uses a versatile 'star' position (hybrid safety/nickel) — the same role Derwin James and Jalen Ramsey played — which Alontae Taylor vacated. A safety with slot coverage ability (like Caleb Downs) has elevated value. Kellen Moore's offense is pass-centric and creative with formations (multiple TE sets with Fant), meaning a WR who can win at all three levels and run after the catch fits best. The Saints ranked 31st in EPA/rush in 2025, so the Etienne signing addresses the run game, but a dynamic pass-catcher is the missing piece for Shough's development.
